Information about the OQS project is available at [https://openquantumsafe.org](https://openquantumsafe.org). The project dashboard is at [https://openquantumsafe.org/dashboard.html](https://openquantumsafe.org/dashboard.html).

## OQS Technical Steering Committee

Rodrigo reported creating a new structure in the [TSC repository](https://github.com/open-quantum-safe/tsc) to store quarterly health status reports in PDF format (merged as [PR 274](https://github.com/open-quantum-safe/tsc/pull/274)). An issue ([#275](https://github.com/open-quantum-safe/tsc/issues/275)) was opened to collect team feedback on the **March 2026 health report**. All team members are asked to review the report and comment on the associated issue.

The team also discussed conference presence. Issues [#276](https://github.com/open-quantum-safe/tsc/issues/276) and [#277](https://github.com/open-quantum-safe/tsc/issues/277) were opened to gather input on target venues and messaging respectively. Upcoming proposal deadlines at the end of the month were flagged for the **OpenSSL** and **PKI Consortium** conferences. Team members are asked to provide input before the end of next week.

> [!IMPORTANT]
> **Action (All):** Review the March 2026 health report and comment on [tsc#275](https://github.com/open-quantum-safe/tsc/issues/275).

> [!IMPORTANT]
> **Action (All):** Review and comment on the conference messaging and venues issues ([tsc#276](https://github.com/open-quantum-safe/tsc/issues/276), [tsc#277](https://github.com/open-quantum-safe/tsc/issues/277)) before end of next week to allow timely conference proposal submissions.

## liboqs

### PR [#2407](https://github.com/open-quantum-safe/liboqs/pull/2407) — Update HQC implementation to 2025-08-22 version

Bruce reported that the PR integrating the official HQC reference implementation is ready for review. Non-trivial changes include namespacing subroutines, replacing the FIPS 202 module, and compiling multiple algorithm variants. All test cases and NIST KATs have passed. This is considered the **last remaining blocker for the liboqs 0.16 release**.

Bruce will reach out to the original HQC submitters starting next Monday to incorporate the necessary code changes and integration metadata, and to determine whether they are willing to take over maintenance of the HQC integration after 0.16. The team agreed this handoff will serve as a useful test of the HQC team's interest in ongoing integration with the OQS stack. Norman raised a question about whether some changes were too OQS-specific; Rodrigo and Bruce clarified that changes such as multi-variant compilation and namespacing are broadly beneficial and not OQS-specific.

> [!IMPORTANT]
> **Action (Bruce):** Contact the original HQC submitters to discuss incorporating code changes and integration metadata, and determine whether they are willing to maintain the HQC integration in liboqs post-0.16. Starting next Monday.

### PR [#2415](https://github.com/open-quantum-safe/liboqs/pull/2415) — Add CodeQL query to enforce OpenSSL return code handling

This PR appears to correctly address the associated issue ([#1867](https://github.com/open-quantum-safe/liboqs/issues/1867)), but no one present had sufficient CodeQL expertise to fully review it. Michael or Rodrigo will identify the original contributor or another knowledgeable reviewer; if none is available, a comment will be left on the PR.

> [!IMPORTANT]
> **Action (Michael / Rodrigo):** Identify a CodeQL-knowledgeable reviewer for PR [#2415](https://github.com/open-quantum-safe/liboqs/pull/2415), or leave a comment on the PR if none is available.

## OQS-OpenSSL 3 provider

Property query improvements for KEM and SIG algorithm lookups were merged (see [PR #774](https://github.com/open-quantum-safe/oqs-provider/pull/774) — Preserve the property query string and [PR #776](https://github.com/open-quantum-safe/oqs-provider/pull/776)).

Regarding **MQOM** inclusion ([issue #777](https://github.com/open-quantum-safe/oqs-provider/issues/777)): if a PR is not submitted within a reasonable timeframe, Rodrigo will take charge of including the algorithm to ensure it is present in the next provider release, aligned with liboqs 0.16.

> [!IMPORTANT]
> **Action (Rodrigo):** If no MQOM PR is submitted in a reasonable timeframe, include MQOM in oqs-provider ahead of the 0.16-aligned release.

## OQS-OpenSSH

Alex is working on an uplift of OQS-OpenSSH to align with the current upstream OpenSSH release. Work on additional PQC key format types (certification of PQC keys) is also planned but has been delayed due to other priorities. Both items remain on the roadmap and are expected to be addressed within a few weeks.

> [!IMPORTANT]
> **Action (Alex):** Uplift OQS-OpenSSH to align with upstream OpenSSH and, when time allows, address PQC key certification.

## OQS-BoringSSL, oqs-demos, ci-containers, www, language wrappers

No updates were raised for these subprojects.
